Workshops

  • HPlan

Workshop Program

The workshops are intended to provide a forum to discuss research advances on a selected topic in an informal setting and to promote dissemination and collaboration on new Planning and Scheduling challenges. Specific details for the individual workshops will be provided in the near future.

Workshop and Tutorial Schedule

  • All Days
  • June 13th
  • June 14th
  • June 15th
  • June 16th
  • June 17th

Accepted Workshops

  • HPlan: Hierarchical Planning
    Pascal Bercher, Jane Jean Kiam, Arthur Bit-Monnot, Ron Alford
    Contact: pascal.bercher@anu.edu.au
  • PRL: Bridging the Gap Between AI Planning and Reinforcement Learning
    MIchael Katz, Hector Palacios, Vincenc Gomez
    Contact: michael.katz1@ibm.com
  • RDDPS: Reliable Data-Driven Planning and Scheduling
    Jörg Hoffmann, Sara Bernardini, Alan Fern, Michele Lombardi, Hector Palacios, Scott Sanner, Marcel Steinmetz, Sylvie Thiebaux
    Contact: hoffmann@cs.uni-saarland.de
  • PlanRob: Planning and Robotics
    Iman Awaad, Alberto Finzi, Andrea Orlandini
    Contact: andrea.orlandini@istc.cnr.it
  • XAIP: Workshop on Explainable AI Planning
    Sarath Sreedharan, Rebecca Eifler, Alan Lindsay, Silvia Tulli, Benjamin Krarup, Stylianos Loukas Vasileiou
    Contact: ssreedh3@asu.edu
  • IntEx: Integrated Planning, Acting, and Execution
    Wiktor Piotrowski, Sunandita Patra, Mark "Mak" Roberts, Tiago Vaquero
    Contact: wiktorpi@parc.com
  • FinPlan: Planning for Financial Services
    Parisa Zehtabi, Alberto Pozanco, Shirin Sohrabi
    Contact: ssohrab@us.ibm.com
  • DAPSPAC: Deception Against Planning Systems and Planning in Adversarial Conditions
    Lukas Chrpa, Mauro Vallati, Ron Alford, Andy Applebaum
    Contact: ralford@mitre.org
  • HSDIP: Heuristics and Search for Domain-independent Planning
    Liat Cohen, Patrick Ferber, Daniel Fiser, Michael Katz, Christian Muise
    Contact: patrick.ferber@unibas.ch
  • SPARK: Scheduling and Planning Applications
    Marco Roveri, Simon Parkinson, Riccardo DeBenedictis, Sabine Storandt
    Contact: marco.roveri@unitn.it
  • KEPS: Knowledge Engineering for Planning and Scheduling
    Lukas Chrpa, Ron Petrick, Mauro Vallati, Tiago Vaquero
    Contact: M.Vallati@hud.ac.uk

ICAPS is the premier forum for exchanging news and research results on theory and applications of intelligent planning and scheduling technology.

© 2022 ICAPS
